title: Build FabricError taxonomy (5 sub-trees)
description: Foundational typed-error base. New packages/shared/src/errors/ with FabricError abstract base + 5 sub-trees: ConfigError / RuleError / IOFabricError / MCPError / InitError. Each sub-tree carries default httpStatus (overridable per subclass — e.g. PathEscapeError extends IOFabricError sets 403). actionHint REQUIRED at construction (empty string disallowed) — the typed code + actionHint contract is what AI consumers read structured warnings from in R28/R24. Migration of 3 existing typed Error classes happens in TASK-010 (error collapse).
scope: Layer 0 infrastructure — typed-error base consumed by error collapse, rule-sync, payload guard, R24 action hints

files.change: NEW: abstract class FabricError extends Error { code: string; actionHint: string; fixable: boolean; details?: unknown; constructor enforces actionHint non-empty }
NEW: class ConfigError extends FabricError { httpStatus = 400 }
NEW: class RuleError extends FabricError { httpStatus = 422 }
NEW: class IOFabricError extends FabricError { httpStatus = 500 } + named subclass PathEscapeError (httpStatus = 403)
NEW: class MCPError extends FabricError { httpStatus = 500 }
NEW: class InitError extends FabricError { httpStatus = 500 }
NEW: barrel re-export of all error classes
NEW: tests for actionHint required / httpStatus default / sub-tree inheritance / instanceof checks

criteria: FabricError construction throws if actionHint is empty string or undefined
Each sub-tree has its default httpStatus; PathEscapeError overrides to 403
All sub-trees serialize via toJSON to { name, code, message, actionHint, fixable, details? } — used by R24 / R28 structured warnings
